task_completion.description: Verify the SEO spider tool is set to List Mode and the target URLs are entered.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an SEO spider tool (e.g., Screaming Frog) with its 'Mode' explicitly set to 'List', and a dialog or window showing the 15 target URLs from the three domains being pasted or uploaded.

task_completion.description: Verify the crawl has been executed and completed for the provided URLs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the SEO spider tool showing a completed crawl (e.g., 100% progress bar) with the target URLs from books.toscrape.com, quotes.toscrape.com, and crawler-test.com visible in the main results table.

task_completion.description: Verify the crawl data is exported to the specified CSV file path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a file manager, terminal window, or save dialog confirming the creation of 'competitive_benchmark.csv' exactly in the '/home/ga/Documents/SEO/exports/' directory.

task_completion.description: Verify the report evaluates the domains based on the required metrics.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor displaying 'competitive_report.txt' with content that explicitly discusses and compares the three domains on: average title length, meta description availability, and response time.

task_completion.description: Verify the report includes at least 3 actionable recommendations and is saved to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the text editor showing 'competitive_report.txt' saved in the '/home/ga/Documents/SEO/reports/' directory (visible in the title bar or save dialog) and containing a clear list of at least 3 actionable SEO recommendations.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used a genuine SEO spider tool GUI to perform the crawl.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the GUI of an SEO spider tool (like Screaming Frog) actively crawling or displaying results, not just a terminal script or a manually typed CSV file.

integrity.description: Verify the crawl results show authentic HTTP status codes for the non-existent URLs.
integrity.visual_evidence: The SEO spider tool's result table should show '404' or 'Not Found' status codes for the fake crawler-test.com URLs (like /titles/long_title and /headers/h1), proving a real network crawl occurred.

integrity.description: Verify the report was written in a text editor rather than generated blindly via terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: A GUI text editor (like gedit, mousepad, or nano in terminal) should be visible with the report content being actively drafted or viewed, showing the agent actually processed the data.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected domains are books.toscrape.com, quotes.toscrape.com, and crawler-test.com. The export path must be exactly /home/ga/Documents/SEO/exports/competitive_benchmark.csv and the report path must be /home/ga/Documents/SEO/reports/competitive_report.txt. The CSV should contain at least 12 rows of crawled data. Note that some specific URL paths for crawler-test.com (e.g., /titles/long_title, /headers/h1) do not actually exist and will legitimately return HTTP 404 errors during the crawl; this is expected behavior and proves the crawl was genuine.
